## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Queensland&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Culture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/EKKA.jpg/250px-EKKA.jpg)](/wiki/File:EKKA.jpg)

The Ekka (the [Royal Queensland Exhibition](/wiki/Ekka "Ekka")) is held each August at the Brisbane Showgrounds.

Queensland is home to major art galleries including the [Queensland Art Gallery](/wiki/Queensland_Art_Gallery "Queensland Art Gallery") and the [Queensland Gallery of Modern Art](/wiki/Queensland_Gallery_of_Modern_Art "Queensland Gallery of Modern Art") as well as cultural institutions such as the [Queensland Ballet](/wiki/Queensland_Ballet "Queensland Ballet"), [Opera Queensland](/wiki/Opera_Queensland "Opera Queensland"), [Queensland Theatre Company](/wiki/Queensland_Theatre_Company "Queensland Theatre Company"), and [Queensland Symphony Orchestra](/wiki/Queensland_Symphony_Orchestra "Queensland Symphony Orchestra"), all based at the [Queensland Cultural Centre](/wiki/Queensland_Cultural_Centre "Queensland Cultural Centre") in Brisbane. The state is the origin of musicians such as the [Bee Gees](/wiki/Bee_Gees "Bee Gees"), [the Go-Betweens](/wiki/The_Go-Betweens "The Go-Betweens"), [the Veronicas](/wiki/The_Veronicas "The Veronicas"), [the Saints](/wiki/The_Saints_(Australian_band) "The Saints (Australian band)"), [Savage Garden](/wiki/Savage_Garden "Savage Garden"), and [Sheppard](/wiki/Sheppard_(band) "Sheppard (band)") as well as writers such as [David Malouf](/wiki/David_Malouf "David Malouf"), [Nick Earls](/wiki/Nick_Earls "Nick Earls") and [Li Cunxin](/wiki/Li_Cunxin "Li Cunxin").

Major annual cultural events include the [Royal Queensland Exhibition](/wiki/Ekka "Ekka") (known locally as the Ekka), an agricultural exhibition held each August at the [Brisbane Showgrounds](/wiki/Brisbane_Showgrounds "Brisbane Showgrounds") as well as the [Brisbane Festival](/wiki/Brisbane_Festival "Brisbane Festival"), which includes one of the nation's largest annual fireworks displays called 'Riverfire', and which is held each September.

### Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Queensland&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Sport")]

Main article: [Sport in Queensland](/wiki/Sport_in_Queensland "Sport in Queensland")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/The_Gabba_Panorama.jpg/250px-The_Gabba_Panorama.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Gabba_Panorama.jpg)

[Cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") game at [The Gabba](/wiki/The_Gabba "The Gabba"), a 42,000-seat round stadium in [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane")

The state of Queensland is represented in all of Australia's national sporting competitions and it is also host to a number of domestic and international sporting events. The most popular winter and summer team sports are [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") and [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket"), respectively.

In the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"), the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos"), [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys"), [The Dolphins](/wiki/Dolphins_(NRL) "Dolphins (NRL)") and [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") are based in the state. Rugby league's annual [State of Origin series](/wiki/State_of_Origin_series "State of Origin series") is a major event in the Queensland sporting calendar, with the [Queensland Maroons](/wiki/Queensland_rugby_league_team "Queensland rugby league team") representing the state.

In cricket, the [Queensland Bulls](/wiki/Queensland_Bulls "Queensland Bulls") represent the state in the [Sheffield Shield](/wiki/Sheffield_Shield "Sheffield Shield") and the [Ryobi One Day Cup](/wiki/Ryobi_One_Day_Cup "Ryobi One Day Cup"), while the [Brisbane Heat](/wiki/Brisbane_Heat "Brisbane Heat") compete in the [Big Bash League](/wiki/Big_Bash_League "Big Bash League").

Queensland is also home to the [Brisbane Lions](/wiki/Brisbane_Lions "Brisbane Lions") and the [Gold Coast Suns](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Suns "Gold Coast Suns") in the [Australian Football League](/wiki/Australian_Football_League "Australian Football League") ([Australian rules football](/wiki/Australian_rules_football "Australian rules football")), and the [Brisbane Roar FC](/wiki/Brisbane_Roar_FC "Brisbane Roar FC") in the [A-League](/wiki/A-League "A-League") (soccer). In netball, the [Queensland Firebirds](/wiki/Queensland_Firebirds "Queensland Firebirds") went undefeated in the 2011 season as they went on to win the Grand Final. Other sports teams are the [Brisbane Bullets](/wiki/Brisbane_Bullets "Brisbane Bullets") and the [Cairns Taipans](/wiki/Cairns_Taipans "Cairns Taipans"), who compete in the [National Basketball League](/wiki/National_Basketball_League_(Australia) "National Basketball League (Australia)").

The state is represented by the [Queensland Reds](/wiki/Queensland_Reds "Queensland Reds") in the [Super Rugby](/wiki/Super_Rugby "Super Rugby") (rugby union).

Swimming is also a popular sport in Queensland, with many Australian team members and international medalists hailing from the state.

Brisbane will host the [2032 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2032_Summer_Olympics "2032 Summer Olympics"), marking the third time Australia hosted the Olympic Games following [Melbourne 1956](/wiki/1956_Summer_Olympics "1956 Summer Olympics") and [Sydney 2000](/wiki/2000_Summer_Olympics "2000 Summer Olympics").[[125]](#cite_note-135) Major recurring sporting events hosted in Queensland include: the [Gold Coast 600](/wiki/Gold_Coast_600 "Gold Coast 600") (motorsport; since 1994), the [Gold Coast Marathon](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Marathon "Gold Coast Marathon") (athletics; since 1979), the [NRL All Stars Game](/wiki/NRL_All_Stars_Game "NRL All Stars Game") (rugby league; since 2010), the [Townsville 400](/wiki/Townsville_400 "Townsville 400") (motorsport; since 2009), the [Quicksilver Pro and Roxy Pro](/wiki/World_championship_tour_(WCT)_surfing "World championship tour (WCT) surfing") (surfing) and [Australian PGA Championship](/wiki/Australian_PGA_Championship "Australian PGA Championship") (golf; since 2000).

### Symbols and emblems

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Queensland&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Symbols and emblems")]

Main article: [Symbols of Queensland](/wiki/Symbols_of_Queensland "Symbols of Queensland")

The official state emblems of Queensland are prescribed in the Emblems of Queensland Act 2005.

[Queen Victoria](/wiki/Queen_Victoria "Queen Victoria") granted the Queensland Coat of Arms to the Colony of Queensland in 1893, making it the oldest State Arms in Australia.[[126]](#cite_note-:0-136) It depicts Queensland's primary industries in the 19th century with a sheaf of wheat, the heads of a bull and a ram, and a column of gold rising from a heap of quartz. Two stalks of sugar cane which surround the state badge at the top, and below is Queensland's state motto, *Audax at Fidelis*, which means "Bold but Faithful". In 1977, [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II") granted the supporting animals, the [brolga](/wiki/Brolga "Brolga") and the [red deer](/wiki/Red_deer "Red deer").[[126]](#cite_note-:0-136)[[127]](#cite_note-:1-137)

In November 2003 [maroon](/wiki/Maroon "Maroon") was officially named Queensland's state colour, after many years of association with Queensland sporting teams. In 2025, Queensland Government branding changed to blue, with not yet an impact on the official state colour.[[128]](#cite_note-138)

The [koala](/wiki/Koala "Koala") was officially named the animal or faunal, emblem of Queensland in 1971 after a newspaper poll showed strong public support. The Queensland Government introduced the poll due to a proposal by state tourism ministers for all states to adopt a faunal emblem.[[126]](#cite_note-:0-136) In January 1986, the brolga was announced as the official bird emblem of Queensland, after many years on the Coat of Arms.[[127]](#cite_note-:1-137)

The [Cooktown orchid](/wiki/Dendrobium_bigibbum "Dendrobium bigibbum") became known as Queensland's floral emblem in 1959, during celebrations to mark the state's centenary,[[129]](#cite_note-139)[[130]](#cite_note-140) and the [Barrier Reef Anemone Fish](/wiki/Amphiprion_akindynos "Amphiprion akindynos") was officially named as Queensland's aquatic emblem in March 2005.[[131]](#cite_note-141)

The [sapphire](/wiki/Sapphire "Sapphire") was named the official state gem for Queensland in August 1985.[[132]](#cite_note-142)[[133]](#cite_note-143)

### Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Queensland&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Transport")]

Queensland is served by several [National Highways](/wiki/National_Highway_(Australia) "National Highway (Australia)") and, particularly in South East Queensland, a network of freeways such as the [M1](/wiki/M1_(Queensland) "M1 (Queensland)"). The [Department of Transport & Main Roads](/wiki/Department_of_Transport_and_Main_Roads "Department of Transport and Main Roads") oversees the development and operation of main roads and public transport, including taxis and local aviation.

[Principal rail services](/wiki/Rail_transport_in_Queensland "Rail transport in Queensland") are provided by [Queensland Rail](/wiki/Queensland_Rail "Queensland Rail"), predominantly between the major centres east of the Great Dividing Range. Freight rail services in Queensland have been provided mostly by [Aurizon](/wiki/Aurizon "Aurizon") and [Pacific National](/wiki/Pacific_National "Pacific National"), with interstate intermodal services provided by Pacific National and [SCT Logistics](/wiki/SCT_Logistics "SCT Logistics"). Major seaports include the [Port of Brisbane](/wiki/Port_of_Brisbane "Port of Brisbane"), Australia's third busiest by value of goods, as well as those at [Gladstone](/wiki/Gladstone,_Queensland "Gladstone, Queensland"), [Townsville](/wiki/Townsville "Townsville"), and [Bundaberg](/wiki/Bundaberg "Bundaberg"). There are large coal export facilities at [Hay Point](/wiki/Hay_Point,_Queensland "Hay Point, Queensland"), Gladstone, and [Abbot Point](/wiki/Abbot_Point "Abbot Point"). Major sugar export facilities are located at [Lucinda](/wiki/Lucinda,_Queensland "Lucinda, Queensland") and [Mackay](/wiki/Mackay,_Queensland "Mackay, Queensland").

[Brisbane Airport](/wiki/Brisbane_Airport "Brisbane Airport") is the main international and domestic gateway serving the state, and is the [third busiest in Australia](/wiki/List_of_the_busiest_airports_in_Australia "List of the busiest airports in Australia"). Other international airports include the [Gold Coast Airport](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Airport "Gold Coast Airport"), [Cairns International Airport](/wiki/Cairns_International_Airport "Cairns International Airport"), and [Townsville Airport](/wiki/Townsville_Airport "Townsville Airport"). Regional airports with scheduled domestic flights include [Toowoomba Wellcamp Airport](/wiki/Toowoomba_Wellcamp_Airport "Toowoomba Wellcamp Airport"), [Great Barrier Reef Airport](/wiki/Great_Barrier_Reef_Airport "Great Barrier Reef Airport"), [Hervey Bay Airport](/wiki/Hervey_Bay_Airport "Hervey Bay Airport"), [Bundaberg Airport](/wiki/Bundaberg_Airport "Bundaberg Airport"), [Mackay Airport](/wiki/Mackay_Airport "Mackay Airport"), [Mount Isa Airport](/wiki/Mount_Isa_Airport "Mount Isa Airport"), [Proserpine / Whitsunday Coast Airport](/wiki/Whitsunday_Coast_Airport "Whitsunday Coast Airport"), [Rockhampton Airport](/wiki/Rockhampton_Airport "Rockhampton Airport"), and [Sunshine Coast Airport](/wiki/Sunshine_Coast_Airport "Sunshine Coast Airport").

[South East Queensland](/wiki/South_East_Queensland "South East Queensland") has an integrated public transport system operated by [Translink](/wiki/TransLink_(South_East_Queensland) "TransLink (South East Queensland)"), which provides services [bus](/wiki/Bus_transport_in_Queensland "Bus transport in Queensland"), [rail](/wiki/Queensland_Rail_City_network "Queensland Rail City network"), [light rail](/wiki/G:link "G:link") and [Brisbane's ferry services](/wiki/RiverCity_Ferries "RiverCity Ferries") through Queensland Rail and contracted operators. The region is divided into seven [Fare](/wiki/Fare "Fare") zones radiating outwards from the [Brisbane central business district](/wiki/Brisbane_central_business_district "Brisbane central business district"), which is the central hub for the system. The [Queensland Rail City network](/wiki/Queensland_Rail_City_network "Queensland Rail City network") consists of 152 train stations along 13 suburban rail lines and across the region, and predominantly within Brisbane's metropolitan area. There is also a large bus network including Brisbane's large dedicated [bus rapid transit](/wiki/Bus_rapid_transit "Bus rapid transit") network, the [Brisbane busway network](/wiki/Busways_in_Brisbane "Busways in Brisbane"). Brisbane's popular [ferry services](/wiki/RiverCity_Ferries "RiverCity Ferries") include the CityCat, Cross River, and CityHopper services which have dedicated wharves along the [Brisbane River](/wiki/Brisbane_River "Brisbane River"). The [G:link](/wiki/G:link "G:link"), Queensland's only [light rail](/wiki/Light_rail "Light rail") network, operates on the Gold Coast.[[134]](#cite_note-144)

The new Queensland [Cross River Rail](/wiki/Cross_River_Rail "Cross River Rail") is a metro network that is currently under development within [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane") and is part of infrastructure to prepare the city for the [2032 Olympic games](/wiki/2032_Olympic_Games "2032 Olympic Games").[[135]](#cite_note-145)

### Other utilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Queensland&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Other utilities")]

[Queensland Health](/wiki/Queensland_Health "Queensland Health") operates and administers the state's [public health](/wiki/Public_health "Public health") system. There are sixteen regional Health and Hospital Services corresponding to geographical regions which are responsible for delivering public health services within their regions. Major public hospitals include the [Royal Brisbane and Women's Hospital](/wiki/Royal_Brisbane_and_Women%27s_Hospital "Royal Brisbane and Women's Hospital"), [Princess Alexandra Hospital](/wiki/Princess_Alexandra_Hospital,_Brisbane "Princess Alexandra Hospital, Brisbane"), the [Mater Hospital](/wiki/Mater_Group "Mater Group"), the [Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Hospital](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Jubilee_Hospital "Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Hospital"), and the [Queensland Children's Hospital](/wiki/Queensland_Children%27s_Hospital "Queensland Children's Hospital") in Brisbane, as well as the [Townsville University Hospital](/wiki/Townsville_University_Hospital "Townsville University Hospital"), [Cairns Hospital](/wiki/Cairns_Hospital "Cairns Hospital"), [Gold Coast Hospital](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Hospital "Gold Coast Hospital") and [Gold Coast University Hospital](/wiki/Gold_Coast_University_Hospital "Gold Coast University Hospital") in the regional cities. There are smaller public hospitals, as well as private hospitals, around the state.
